host: hetzner (anima-ax102, AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D, 128 GB RAM, no GPU)
config: training/config/clm_170m.json (d=768 L=12 H=8 KV=4 blk=512 batch=2 lr=3e-4 steps=1 save_every=1)

symptom_observed_by_prior_agent.exit_code: silent (process exit observed without signal, OOM, or crash log)
symptom_observed_by_prior_agent.diagnosis_at_time: silent abort inside train_step() step=0, suspect _c41_forward_aux / quantum_controller_step

actual_root_cause.summary: No abort existed. Two orthogonal hexa issues compound into an appearance of silent exit.

contributors.description: hexa AOT C codegen drops variadic args in `println(str, val, str, val, ...)` multi-arg form. Only the FIRST string literal is emitted; every subsequent int/float/string argument is silently not printed. Affects the step-log line at train_clm.hexa:3510-3515 and the [sw] line at 3521-3535, plus six bisect probes added during diagnosis that exhibited the same pattern (verified by capturing `[bisect] T0 train_step enter step=` with no step value, etc.).
contributors.consequence: Observer never sees any step/CE/phi metric value — the line containing these numbers is printed, but as a bare string terminated at the first variadic position. Subjectively the stdout looks 'frozen' past init.

contributors.description: save_checkpoint() at train_clm.hexa:908 serialises the full 170M-param model as HEXACKPT-v1 TEXT via _ckpt_append_tensor (L886). Inner loop builds a string via repeated `batch = batch + ' ' + str(data[i])` — O(_CKPT_BATCH²) inside the inner loop, and 170M/_CKPT_BATCH (=64) ≈ 2.66M append_file() syscalls outer. On hetzner CPU the entire save takes ~6 min for 170M (measured: 1.15 GB text ckpt written at ~2.3 MB/s in post-fix re-run; 518 MB reached after 4.5 min in mid-run).
contributors.consequence: After step 0 completes, the program spends 6 min in the save routine. Observer watching the 11m35s run saw no new output for >10 min (because of contributor A, no step line either) and assumed abort.

contributors.description: save_checkpoint uses atomic tmp→rename (.tmp + rename() at end). Mid-save the file lives at /tmp/ckpt_clm170m_smoke/step_0.tmp; the final step_0 only appears at save completion.
contributors.consequence: Observer's `ls /tmp/ckpt_clm170m_smoke/` during mid-save showed an empty directory (because the `.tmp` file is in a sibling path under the per-step subdir OR the rotation cleanup had already run).

bisect_trail: v1 bisect build: added println probes T0..T8 in train_step and A0..A8 in _c41_forward_aux and P0..P6 in _c41_phi_holo. Also DM0..DM6 in decoder_model_new and I0..I2 in train_scale_full.

v1 run: tail of /tmp/bisect_v1.log showed bisect probes at TOP of file (printed eagerly or in parallel) then normal output ending at `[train_scale] init begin decoder_model_new size=768x12` — same apparent crash site as prior smoke.

v2 run (after kill+LD_LIBRARY_PATH fix): tail showed all bisect probes ran through to `[bisect] T8 _c41_forward_aux done phi_holo=` and `[train_scale] step=` then apparent stall. ps --ppid showed RES=12.9 GB, stat=R, %CPU=270 — PROCESS WAS ACTIVELY RUNNING, not aborted.
v2 continuation: /tmp/ckpt_clm170m_bisect/step_0.tmp grew from 132 MB → 157 MB → 518 MB → 1.15 GB over 6 min total, then renamed to step_0. Exit code 0.
Conclusion: zero silent abort. The 'silent exit' was diagnostic-blind slow save.

fix_applied.change: Replaced multi-arg `println(str, var, str, var, ...)` calls in the step log and [sw] diagnostic log with explicit `+ str(...)` string concatenation. Mirrors the already-working pattern at line 3469 (`[train_scale] cfg effective batch=...`). No codegen or interpreter change needed — uses hexa features that already work.
fix_applied.inline_comment: Added troubleshoot-comment block citing this bug_id as root cause.
not_fixed_here: _ckpt_append_tensor O(N²) text-format serialisation (contributor B) — out of scope for r5-a6 (BLAS coverage r5-a7 or a binary ckpt rewrite is the proper fix). Workaround: for smoke use --save-every >= total_steps so the save only fires at the end, OR accept the 6-min overhead.

hexa println multi-arg codegen bug itself (contributor A root) — filed implicitly by this bug JSON; requires hexa compiler patch, not anima patch. All anima call sites should prefer `+ str(...)` concatenation until codegen is fixed.

verification.build: NO_HEXA_SHIM=1 /root/.hx/bin/hexa build training/train_clm.hexa -o /tmp/clm_v2.bin (BUILD_EXIT=0, 3 warnings, 428 KB binary)
verification.smoke_expected_log_line_post_fix: [train_scale] step=0 loss=<float> phase=<label> phi_holo=<float> phi_gwt=<float> l_holo=<float> l_gwt=<float>
verification.smoke_result: PENDING — launched in background, waiting for ~6 min save to finish. See /tmp/smoke_fix.log on hetzner.

memory: feedback_hexa_silent_exit_5_imports — this bug family was suspected but a different root cause applies here
